Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Protective Order Directing That Deposition Occur By Written Question (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Mar-15-2022 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Protective Order Directing That Deposition Occur By Written Question
Off Calendar Mar-15-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JENELLE WELLING, Esq. of Infinity Law Group LLP (Tel: 925-732-1188) for plaintiff Pavel Safaniev;
Atty. JODY A. LANDRY, Esq. of Littler Mendelson, P.C. (Tel: 619-232-0441) for defendants Deluxe Corporation, and Deluxe Small Business Sales, Inc.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF PAVEL SAFANIEV's Motion For Entry Of Protective Order Directing That Deposition Occur By Written Question.

Parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for parties (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion for protective order DENIED. First, to the extent Plaintiff anticipates testifying at trial, Defendants would be prejudiced by denying the opportunity to observe him testify because his presentation as a witness will be key in assessing his credibility and to evaluating settlement options or planning for trial. Second, it is unclear that the medical providers understood that a deposition by written question still requires the deposition officer to read the questions to the deponent orally and take down that live testimony. See the deposition testimony of Dr. Grinberg, submitted with the reply. Third, alternative means exist to protect Plaintiff. Defendants agree to such steps as limiting the time of each deposition session and to taking it remotely. I encourage the parties to meet-and-confer and agree to a formal protocol to govern the oral deposition. If necessary, Plaintiff may suspend the deposition and seek relief from the Court, including potentially a shift to deposition by written questions. At this point, though, Plaintiff has not demonstrated that deposition by written questions is necessary.

Counsel for defendants to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/JPT)
